transcript: An Encino woman comes face-to-face with a burglary crew who broke into her home and the frightening encounter is captured on a ring security camera. The break-in is just the latest in a surge in burglaries reported in Encino. KTLA's Carlos Salcedo is live in Encino with more on the story for us tonight. Carlos. Mike and Cher, at least two of these break-ins have been caught on camera, but police say there have been dozens more of these home burglaries in the Encino area. Tonight, homeowners tell me that they're worried these suspects could strike again. Mass bandits trying to hide their tracks without realizing they were being watched the whole time. The home burglary happened earlier this month during the middle of the day. Surveillance cameras around the Encino home captured the burglars every move, from peeping through a sliding glass door to smashing a doorway. Two suspects in hooded sweaters and face masks make entry, tiptoeing to the upstairs of the home. It's unclear what they took, but as the two men walked out the front door, one of them puts his hand over the ring camera attempting to rotate it from view. A similar incident happened last Friday at an Encino Hills home. The homeowners were inside at the time and scare off the intruders. Four of them are seen running off dressed in similar attire. I yelled and screamed and so they didn't want to cause any trouble. I think they just wanted to run. The wife and husband did not want to be identified, but they came face to face with the burglars. We did not see any weapons in their hand, but they all had backpacks. Nothing was taken and no one was hurt. The suspects came in through the house next door, which is under construction. You see them crawling behind some of the fencing as they eventually sneak into the property. It's unclear if the two incidents are related, but in the latest case, the suspects managed to turn away some of the security cameras. According to police, there have been more than 400 burglaries in the Encino area in the last 12 months. I've been living here for 30 years. I never had this incident happening in our neighborhood. She's, you know, she doesn't feel, none of us feel comfortable. I go to work, I'm worried. And in one case, the suspects were seen leaving in a white Dodge Caravan. So far, police have made no arrests. We're live in Encino tonight. Carlos Salcedo, KTLA 5 News.
